title: rubus × semivelatus
intro: Rubus × semivelatus, also known as the hybrid black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus), and is a type of bramble plant that produces edible fruit. The medicinal properties of Rubus × semivelatus include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of consuming the fruit of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide protection against certain types of cancer. Rubus × semivelatus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
